behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names all key specifics tested: conflicting Content-Length → 400, empty Content-Length → 400, identical Content-Length accepted, scripts/pr-comments.ts file, --json flag, PR number argument, package.json pr:comments entry pointing to pr-comments.ts, CLAUDE.md documentation with pr:comments, JSON schema keys (when, user, kind, location, body, url, resolved, outdated). All assert targets are discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes the desired behavior (reject conflicting Content-Length with 400, create pr-comments.ts, etc.) but does not specify exact code changes, line numbers, or implementation details like the bloom filter check or specific loop structure used in the gold patch.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The HTTP parser tests are behavioral: they send raw TCP requests and check for HTTP 400/200 responses, not implementation internals. The pr-comments tests check file existence, transpilability, argument acceptance, and package.json entries — all of which any correct implementation would satisfy. No gold-specific variable names or internal helpers are asserted.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The HTTP parser tests create actual Bun servers, send raw TCP requests, and verify HTTP response codes. The pr-comments tests transpile and execute the script, check runtime behavior. The package.json test loads JSON and runs 'bun run pr:comments'. The CLAUDE.md test does use text search but also verifies the documented command runs. The majority of tests are execution-based.

test_not_tautological.reason: The HTTP parser tests send actual malicious requests and verify 400 responses — a stub server returning 200 would fail. The pr-comments tests require a parseable TypeScript file that accepts arguments and a --json flag. A stub empty file would fail transpilation or argument checks. These are non-trivial to satisfy without real implementation.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors from the instruction are tested: conflicting CL rejection (400), empty CL rejection (400), identical CL acceptance (200), pr-comments.ts existence and parseability, PR number argument, --json flag, package.json entry, and CLAUDE.md documentation. The JSON schema keys are checked in test_claude_md_documents_pr_comments (truncated but referenced).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_http_parser_rejects_conflicting_duplicate_content_length, test_pr_comments_script_parses, etc.), grouped by concern (HTTP parser behavioral tests vs. PR comments tooling tests), and each has a detailed docstring explaining the RFC reference or rationale.

structured_data_schema.reason: The instruction explicitly documents the JSON output schema keys: when, user, kind, location, body, url, resolved, outdated. The CLAUDE.md test verifies these keys are documented.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames (pr-comments.ts, HttpParser.h, CLAUDE.md, package.json), paths, commands (bun run pr:comments), or variable names throughout the instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names HttpParser.h as a file to look at, which is appropriate for the task domain (HTTP parser fix). It doesn't specify exact line numbers, function names within the file, or the specific implementation approach (bloom filter, loop structure, etc.).

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold patch makes localized edits: appending a section to CLAUDE.md, adding one line to package.json scripts, modifying a specific section of HttpParser.h, and creating a new file. No full-file rewrites or contradictions with other files.
